text: Collaborative filtering recommends items based on the behavior of similar users or items.
explanation: It is one of the classic foundations of recommendation systems.
usage: Used in e-commerce, media, and personalized ranking systems.
spanish: El filtrado colaborativo recomienda ítems a partir del comportamiento de usuarios o ítems similares.

text: Matrix factorization learns latent factors that explain user-item interactions.
explanation: It is still a strong baseline for many ranking problems.
usage: Common in recommender systems and low-latency ranking tasks.
spanish: La factorización de matrices aprende factores latentes que explican interacciones usuario-ítem.

text: Negative sampling trains ranking models by contrasting positive items with a sample of negatives.
explanation: It makes training efficient when the set of possible items is huge.
usage: Used in search, ads, and recommendation ranking.
spanish: El negative sampling entrena modelos de ranking contrastando ítems positivos con una muestra de negativos.

text: Graph neural networks propagate information along edges using message passing.
explanation: This makes them effective for relational and networked data.
usage: Used in social graphs, molecular graphs, and knowledge graphs.
spanish: Las redes neuronales de grafos propagan información a través de aristas usando message passing.

scenario: Graph Learning: Node and Graph Embeddings 🌐
text: Graph embeddings represent nodes or entire graphs in a vector space for downstream learning.
explanation: They enable graph similarity, link prediction, and classification.
usage: Useful in chemistry, fraud detection, and network analysis.
spanish: Los embeddings de grafos representan nodos o grafos completos en un espacio vectorial para aprendizaje downstream.

text: Cold start happens when the system has little or no information about a new user or item.
explanation: It is one of the hardest problems in production recommendation systems.
usage: Critical in new-user onboarding and newly launched products.
spanish: El cold start ocurre cuando el sistema tiene poca o ninguna información sobre un usuario o ítem nuevo.
